meta.title: On Combat
meta.subtitle: The Psychology and Physiology of Deadly Conflict in War and in Peace
meta.author: Lt. Col. Dave Grossman · with Loren W. Christensen

journey.unlocks: The science behind why you train the way you train — and why repetition is not optional.
journey.identity_shift: From person who hopes they’ll perform under pressure — to person who has installed the program they intend to run.

pillars.name: Physiology
pillars.why: This book is the science of what your body does under lethal stress — and it explains exactly why ETKM trains the way it does.

pillars.name: Mindset
pillars.why: The condition code, decision rehearsal, and 'you run the program you installed' are mindset frameworks that change how students approach every training session.

pillars.name: Awareness
pillars.why: Condition Yellow is named, defined, and given a biological basis — this is the science behind the awareness standard ETKM teaches every student from day one.

pillars.name: Recovery
pillars.why: Grossman documents post-incident physiology and recovery — the part of the readiness cycle most self-defense curricula skip entirely.

principles.text: You don’t rise to the occasion. You fall to the level of your training.
Condition Yellow is your permanent baseline in public. Not fear — awareness.
Fine motor skills fail first under high arousal. Train gross motor to reflex.
Train for the body you will have under stress — not the body you have when calm.
Decision rehearsal before the event removes the decision from the event. Write your if-then rules now.
The freeze is biological — and interrupted by a pre-rehearsed first action.
Breathing is a control lever. Four counts in, hold, out, hold. Daily practice builds the skill.
Scan every environment on entry. Exits, cover, anomalies. Every time. No exceptions.
Perceptual distortions under stress are normal — expect them and plan around them.
Recovery is part of readiness. Decompression after hard training is not optional.
Simple beats complicated when the adrenaline hits. Drill fundamentals deep.
Moral clarity before an event protects the mind long after the event is over.

this_week: Start tactical breathing — 5 minutes daily. 4-4-4-4 cadence. Morning or before bed.
Write 5 personal if-then decision rules for situations relevant to your life.
Scan on entry to every new environment today. Every time. No exceptions.
this_month: Expand decision rules to 10. Review them weekly.
Request more stress-inoculation training. Tell your instructor you want more pressure.
After every hard session: 5 minutes of breathing and mental debrief. Build the habit.
permanent: Live in Condition Yellow in public. Know where you are and what is around you.
Trust your trained responses over your in-the-moment instincts.
Treat recovery as the last phase of every readiness cycle — not an afterthought.
